Project Name: BODHI: Medical AI as Epistemic Agent
Description: Systems design framework reimagining medical AI as an epistemic agent: one that questions assumptions, reasons under uncertainty, and recognizes the boundaries of its own knowledge.

Project Name: LabTube: 3D Temporal Modeling of ICU Labs
Description: VideoMAE-style modeling of ICU lab time series. Elevates time to a structural dimension ('the tube'). Unifies time and lab space in one 3D tensor for spatio-temporal modeling. Described as successor to Lab-MAE.
Notes / Duplicates: Related to Lab-MAE (explicit successor). Kept as a separate project.

Description: AI-a-thon for high school students, teachers, data scientists, and healthcare professionals. Cross-disciplinary teams tackle real-world AI-in-healthcare activities. Youth-centered.
Team Members: Ahram Han (https://www.notion.so/Ahram-Han-318e4faff87f8121af18dd304596944a?pvs=21), Takeshi Tohyama (https://www.notion.so/Takeshi-Tohyama-318e4faff87f81e2a9adf4ac0de20d03?pvs=21), Tiger Krittaphas (https://www.notion.so/Tiger-Krittaphas-326e4faff87f806682fae38201d9fc23?pvs=21), Boya Zhang (https://www.notion.so/Boya-Zhang-318e4faff87f810aa3caff0f6c2210d0?pvs=21), Ryohei Yamamoto-Kobayashi (https://www.notion.so/Ryohei-Yamamoto-Kobayashi-318e4faff87f818f884fc9e94807e796?pvs=21), Laurine Sprehe (https://www.notion.so/Laurine-Sprehe-318e4faff87f81d1802bcb1a03d089db?pvs=21), Hannes Ill (https://www.notion.so/Hannes-Ill-318e4faff87f8133a59ed978c7fd44dd?pvs=21), Marlene Louisa Moerig (https://www.notion.so/Marlene-Louisa-Moerig-318e4faff87f8183a1bbd9b8b557ce0f?pvs=21)
Key Team Members: Ahram Han, Ann Larson, Boya Zhang, Doug Rademacher, Hannes Ill, Laurine Sprehe, Ryohei Kobayashi, Takeshi Tohyama, Tiger Krittaphas, Marlene, and others

Project Name: MIT Critical Data Branding, Events & Comms
Description: Coordination of the lab's external events calendar, website (criticaldata.mit.edu), social media (LinkedIn/X/Instagram), YouTube, and communication infrastructure. Includes AI Attitudes platform.
Team Members: Saleem Ameen (https://www.notion.so/Saleem-Ameen-318e4faff87f81f0a6fcc93a5bf3f005?pvs=21), Milit Patel (https://www.notion.so/Milit-Patel-326e4faff87f8022ad72e35c5864db2d?pvs=21), Marlene Louisa Moerig (https://www.notion.so/Marlene-Louisa-Moerig-318e4faff87f8183a1bbd9b8b557ce0f?pvs=21), Aryan Bhattacharya (https://www.notion.so/Aryan-Bhattacharya-326e4faff87f80978eaac1f1b30a5dab?pvs=21)
Key Team Members: Saleem Ameen, Aryan Bhattacharya, Han-Jay Shu, Rahul, Marlene, Mindy, Milit Patel, Akshay Kelshiker, Dukyong

Description: Using the 6 tools framework combined with January 15th event outcomes to summarize desired changes in medical education.
Evaluating AI performance, entropy, and calibration in discordant data across multiple modalities.
Key Team Members: Vector Embedding team, HST.956 class students
Notes / Duplicates: Overlaps with VE team work. Kept as a separate project. Submitted by Takeshi Tohyama.

Description: January 15th 2026 large multi-stakeholder event on AI in healthcare.
Key Team Members: Djillali Annane, Lorna Atimango, Azra Bihorac, + ~100 others

Description: Using Central America critical health data to analyze current healthcare outcomes.
Measuring how pain is documented across clinical notes (BRIDGE) and standardized exam questions (WorldMedQA) to uncover systematic bias in data and models.
Notes / Duplicates: Related to WorldmedQA (Sebastien). Same datasets, same bias theme. Kept as a separate project.

Project Name: SLICES: SSL Objectives for ICU Representation
Description: Modular framework comparing self-supervised learning (SSL) objectives on ICU patient representation tasks. Master's thesis project.
No description provided.
Notes / Duplicates: Description missing � follow up with Sebastian Cajas.

Description: Infrastructure for AI-assisted clinical research enabling natural-language exploration of ICU datasets (MIMIC-IV, eICU). Operates as an MCP server (Claude Desktop, Cursor) and Python API with clinician-authored Agent Skills modules. Evaluation framework: L1 SQL accuracy, L2 clinical artifact generation, L3 research reproduction. Preprint target April 2026.
Team Members: Ahram Han (https://www.notion.so/Ahram-Han-318e4faff87f8121af18dd304596944a?pvs=21), Ryohei Yamamoto-Kobayashi (https://www.notion.so/Ryohei-Yamamoto-Kobayashi-318e4faff87f818f884fc9e94807e796?pvs=21), Marlene Louisa Moerig (https://www.notion.so/Marlene-Louisa-Moerig-318e4faff87f8183a1bbd9b8b557ce0f?pvs=21), Dukyong Yoon (https://www.notion.so/Dukyong-Yoon-318e4faff87f81238d19c0f45634805d?pvs=21), Takeshi Tohyama (https://www.notion.so/Takeshi-Tohyama-318e4faff87f81e2a9adf4ac0de20d03?pvs=21)
Key Team Members: Ahram Han, Ryohei Yamamoto-Kobayashi, Dukyong Yoon, Takeshi Tohyama, Marlene Moerig
Notes / Duplicates: DUPLICATE: Submitted 3x � by Hannes Ill (�2) and Benet Fite Abril. Marlene's entry is most complete and used as canonical.

Project Name: Impact Analysis of Prediction Models (Causal Inference)
Description: Evaluating whether prediction models improve patient outcomes before clinical implementation. Methods: target trial emulation, interrupted time series, difference-in-difference, and other causal inference approaches.
Team Members: Takeshi Tohyama (https://www.notion.so/Takeshi-Tohyama-318e4faff87f81e2a9adf4ac0de20d03?pvs=21), Ahram Han (https://www.notion.so/Ahram-Han-318e4faff87f8121af18dd304596944a?pvs=21), Norman Pedersen (https://www.notion.so/Norman-Pedersen-318e4faff87f81be9a6edf95f2c888c0?pvs=21)

Description: Measuring model drift over time using MIMIC data.
Pipeline to extract vector embeddings from pre-trained transformers. Final stages of v1.
Team Members: Ahram Han (https://www.notion.so/Ahram-Han-318e4faff87f8121af18dd304596944a?pvs=21), Takeshi Tohyama (https://www.notion.so/Takeshi-Tohyama-318e4faff87f81e2a9adf4ac0de20d03?pvs=21), Norman Pedersen (https://www.notion.so/Norman-Pedersen-318e4faff87f81be9a6edf95f2c888c0?pvs=21)
Notes / Duplicates: Related to the broader Vector Embedding team workstream. Kept as a separate project.

Description: Improving snapshot embeddings of EHR data using multimodal data. Version 1 in final stages.
Leveraging the vector embedding approach for integration of multimodal data in cardiovascular disease.

Project Name: XAI Claims in ICU ML Models (Scoping Review)
Description: PRISMA-ScR scoping review systematically mapping, analyzing, and assessing claims about explainability in ML models for diagnostic/prognostic decision-making in intensive care.
Key Team Members: Louis Agha-mir-Salim, Leo Celi, Prof Stefan Haufe, Jana Fehr, Valerie B�rger, Nicolas Frey

Project Name: Epidemiology of Sentiments in Clinical Notes
Description: Curated repository of open-source sentiment analysis tools for healthcare documents (text, audio, video modalities). Maps sentiment patterns in clinical documentation to patient characteristics, outcomes, and resource utilization.
Project Lead: Leo Anthony Celi (https://www.notion.so/Leo-Anthony-Celi-318e4faff87f80859b67c3ea21572d29?pvs=21), Marlene Louisa Moerig (https://www.notion.so/Marlene-Louisa-Moerig-318e4faff87f8183a1bbd9b8b557ce0f?pvs=21)
Key Team Members: Paola Buedo, Max Lange, Rajna, Gaetano Manzo, Andrei Catalin Coman, Marlene Moerig

Project Name: Bias in Omics Data Beyond Non-Representativeness
Description: Systematic review investigating sources of bias across the full lifecycle of OMICS data (genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics). Covers data production, technical/instrumental, computational/analytical, and reporting stages. ~179 articles reviewed.
Key Team Members: Kezia, Marlene, Marianna, Amir Hossein, Mohammad, Jehad, Sebastian, Osama, Yichun
Notes / Duplicates: DUPLICATE: Also submitted by Sebastian Cajas as 'Omics review' (minimal description). Marlene's entry is canonical.

Description: Comparing how concepts like 'pain' are represented across cultures and languages using BRIDGE and WorldMedQA datasets (~30 countries).
Key Team Members: Siena Placino, Connor B, Marlene, Rajiv
Notes / Duplicates: Related to MedScope (Boya). Same datasets, same bias theme. Kept as a separate project.

Project Name: Lab-MAE: Foundation Model for Lab Data
Description: Transformer-based foundation model creating vector embeddings of laboratory test data from MIMIC. Contextualizes lab values (e.g., creatinine 3.6 means something different per patient/time). Provides a knowledge graph of numeric clinical data capturing physiological states.
